name: superpowers-lab
capabilities: Provides documentation structure for lab environment setup
Explains when and how to use the lab environment for AI experimentation
Links to source repository for additional resources
Guides users through safe AI capability testing workflows
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Security audit found no actual vulnerabilities. Static analyzer flagged 4 patterns that are all false positives: (1) URLs at lines 4 and 22 are documentation links to GitHub repositories, not executable network requests; (2) Lines 3 and 20 contain prose text describing the skill, not cryptographic code. The skill consists entirely of markdown documentation with no executable code, no network operations, no file system access, and no external command execution.
valueStatement: Need a safe space to experiment with Claude AI capabilities without risk? This skill provides documentation and patterns for creating a controlled lab environment to test and explore advanced Claude features.
userTitle: Set up your Claude AI lab environment
limitations: Documentation only - does not execute any code or commands
Requires manual setup of lab environment by user
No automated testing or validation features included
External repository link required for full implementation details

useCases.title: AI Developer Testing
useCases.description: Set up a controlled environment to test new Claude AI capabilities before production deployment

useCases.title: Prompt Engineering Research
useCases.description: Create a safe space to experiment with different prompt patterns and observe Claude behavior

useCases.title: Team Training
useCases.description: Establish a learning environment for teams to explore Claude features without affecting production systems

promptTemplates.title: Basic Lab Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up a basic lab environment for testing Claude AI capabilities. What directories and files should I create?
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner setting up first lab environment

promptTemplates.title: Experiment Design
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to test how Claude handles complex multi-step reasoning tasks. Design an experiment structure for my lab environment.

promptTemplates.title: Safety Boundaries
promptTemplates.prompt: What safety boundaries should I implement in my Claude lab environment to prevent unintended consequences during experimentation?
promptTemplates.scenario: User concerned about safe experimentation practices

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Feature Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive testing framework for evaluating Claude Code integration capabilities in isolated lab conditions.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced user building systematic test suite

bestPractices: Always isolate lab experiments from production systems using separate directories and configurations
Document all experiments with clear descriptions of inputs, expected outputs, and actual results
Establish clear success criteria before beginning any capability testing session
antiPatterns: Testing unvalidated prompts directly in production environments
Failing to log experiment parameters and results for reproducibility
Ignoring rate limits and usage quotas when running automated tests

faq.question: What is a Claude AI lab environment?
faq.answer: A lab environment is a controlled, isolated setup where you can safely experiment with Claude AI capabilities without affecting production systems or real users.

faq.question: Do I need special tools to use this skill?
faq.answer: No special tools are required. This skill provides documentation guidance. You only need a standard development environment and access to Claude AI.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for production testing?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for lab and experimentation purposes. For production testing, you should implement additional safety measures and validation processes.

faq.question: Is my experiment data stored or shared?
faq.answer: This skill is documentation only and does not store or transmit any data. All experiments and their results remain entirely on your local system.

faq.question: How do I get started with the lab environment?
faq.answer: Start by creating a dedicated directory for your lab, then use the prompt templates to guide your initial setup and first experiments with Claude.

faq.question: What if I encounter unexpected Claude behavior?
faq.answer: Document the behavior thoroughly including exact prompts used, then analyze patterns. The lab environment is specifically designed for discovering and understanding such behaviors safely.
